title: Dome of the Rock
description: This quiz is for the essay Early period: The Dome of the Rock (Qubbat al-Sakhra).
topic_path: Art of the Islamic world 640 to now

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Dome of the Rock is one of the earliest surviving buildings from the Islamic world.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/13e23a1557fb3ed51bf03704cd9040aa.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The Dome of the Rock dates from the seventh century and is one of the earliest surviving Islamic buildings.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/west-and-central-asia/a/early-period-the-dome-of-the-rock-qubbat-al-sakhra)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Dome of the Rock is a holy site that belongs to which religious traditions?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/13e23a1557fb3ed51bf03704cd9040aa.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Few places are as holy for Christians, Jews and Muslims as the Dome of the Rock.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/west-and-central-asia/a/early-period-the-dome-of-the-rock-qubbat-al-sakhra)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**At the center of the Dome of the Rock is a large rock, which is believed to be the location where [[☃ dropdown 1]] and also the place where the Angel Gabriel took Muhammad after the miraculous night journey from the Kaaba in Mecca.**




![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0a237c3615aaf3e5f7b522bb69a40594.png)
hints:
**At the center of the Dome of the Rock sits a large rock, which is believed to be the location where Abraham was prepared to sacrifice his son Isaac.** 

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/west-and-central-asia/a/early-period-the-dome-of-the-rock-qubbat-al-sakhra)**

choices.content: Adam and Eve ate the fruit of the tree

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the Dome of the Rock?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0a237c3615aaf3e5f7b522bb69a40594.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The Dome of the Rock seems to take its form from the imperial burial places of Roman emperors, such as Augustus or Hadrian. Its circular form and Dome also reference the Church of the Holy Sepulcher in Jerusalem. The Church of the Holy Sepulcher and the Dome of the Rock have domes that are almost identical in size.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/west-and-central-asia/a/early-period-the-dome-of-the-rock-qubbat-al-sakhra)**

choices.content: The Rock is enclosed by two ambulatories and an octagonal exterior wall.
The use of mosaics reflects an artistic tie to the Late Antiquity.

The mosaics in the Dome of the Rock contain no human figures or animals. While Islam does not prohibit the use of figurative art per se, it seems that in religious buildings, this proscription was upheld.
The shape and dome of the building recall the Pantheon in Rome.
